table.empty: No providers. Press A to add.

language.title: Language
language.help: ↑↓/scroll: select | click: pick | double-click/Enter: apply | Esc: back

form.help: Tab/↑↓ switch fields, Enter next, Enter on last field to save, Esc back.
form.token_note: Token is never written to settings.json in plaintext; saved to profile using the current system secure store.

token_hint.empty: Leave blank to set no Token

confirm.unsaved_body: Save, discard, or cancel to continue editing.
confirm.delete_body: Deletion will be written to custom_profiles.json immediately.

tips.text: *Tip: If you use a mouse, click to select your provider.
*Tip: For security, settings only apply when you launch Claude Code from this window. Running `claude` directly in a new terminal won't be affected.
*Tip: Double-click to activate a provider; if you prefer keyboard, just press Enter.
*Tip: See the line showing the current CLI path? That's your working directory. The "Launch Claude Code" button will start Claude Code there.
*Tip: Pressing Esc while editing will ask whether to save, discard, or cancel.
*Tip: Hot tip: your `Key` uses system secure storage first: DPAPI on Windows, system keyring on macOS/Linux.
*Tip: This is a local tool — your keys never leave your computer.
*Tip: To exit, press Ctrl-C twice. Why? The author doesn't know either...
*Tip: If you've been staring at this screen for over 60 seconds, just press C to launch Claude Code — it's been waiting.
*Tip: Don't worry about picking the wrong provider. Just type `csw` in the terminal anytime to switch.
*Tip: Some found the old config window too cryptic, so this version exists.
*Tip: Press M to see model details — you might discover some hidden config you forgot about.
*Tip: Fun fact: the developer uses this tool themselves, so bugs will hit them first.
*Tip: Fun fact: the developer is just a college student tired of the messy API switching process for Claude Code.
*Tip: Ice-cold fact: `csw --apply ProviderName` switches providers without opening the UI — ready to use immediately.
*Tip: Fun fact: `csw --list` outputs all saved provider names and URLs as JSON, perfect for scripting.
*Tip: Fun fact: `csw --run ProviderName claude` temporarily uses that provider's environment to launch Claude without affecting your current selection.
*Tip: Fun fact: `csw --env-json ProviderName` prints the provider's environment variables (except Token) as JSON — great for debugging model name issues.
*Tip: Fun fact: `csw --sanitize-settings` cleans plaintext keys from settings.json and keeps Token data in secure storage.
*Tip: Fun fact: `csw --select-launch filepath` opens the picker, writes the chosen provider name to the specified file, and exits — useful for scripting.
*Tip: If you like these tips, credit the users. If they suck, blame the author.
*Tip: Double-pressing Enter also launches Claude Code.
*Tip: The author really hates salad 🚫🥗

status.switched_secure: Switched to {name} (secure check passed: key not exposed)
status.enable_first: Enable a model with Enter first
